core_mechanism: an auxiliary, region-masked importance-weighting term added to the fitting objective of a learned predictive/generative surrogate of a dynamical system, which up-weights reconstruction of a small designated subset of the state so the surrogate is forced to preserve components that dominate downstream dynamics despite contributing negligibly to the aggregate reconstruction error
brief_reason: The paper's method, CGSReg, is 'an auxiliary pixel reconstruction loss applied to segmented concept regions,' i.e. a masked extra loss term that reweights training of the learned world-model toward a small task-critical region (the ball) whose under-modeling the authors identify as the source of failure.
